header.stance: 美債全面走弱，重新納入通膨、實質利率與供給折價

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開篇主論點

7月7日美債收盤逆轉前一交易日由殖利率曲線中段領漲的反彈，並使整條殖利率曲線重新反映全面性的通膨與供給折價。10年期殖利率收於4.55%，30年期收於5.05%，2年至30年期券種殖利率約上升6至7個基點。這既不是單純的成長預期上修，也不是地緣政治壓力引發的典型避險反應，而是能源衝擊再起、大量公債與公司債發行，以及更高的實質利率門檻共同造成的同步拋售。

這改變了市場從7月6日延續而來的短期解讀。服務業活動及物流壓力降溫，先前曾使5年期與10年期實質殖利率回落，即使長債仍受到期限溢價約束。週二抹除了中期券種的這波喘息。荷莫茲海峽周邊再度發生攻擊，推升油價並重新引發疑慮：能源通膨下降可能只是暫時現象。WTI收於$70.44；與此同時，紐約Fed調查顯示家庭對未來1年及3年的物價預期上升，進一步增添通膨動能。

供給放大了總體重新定價。Amazon意外進行巨額發債，在市場原已準備迎接美債附息券標售之際，再增加長天期公司債存續期間供給。該公司完成交易，但訂單簿大幅縮減且新券讓利偏高，顯示即使是頂級科技業借款人，市場承接能力仍有極限。3年期美債標售投標強勁，限制了將此解讀為全面性需求失靈的空間，卻未能阻止指標券及長端殖利率突破支撐前一交易日收盤的水準。

走勢的組成顯示實質利率居於主導地位。10年期實質殖利率上升6個基點，損益兩平通膨率則僅上升1個基點，因此油價衝擊並未轉化為失控的通膨預期變動。面對Fed路徑不確定、大量發債及跨市場壓力，投資人轉而要求更高的實質補償，才願意持有存續期間。日本強化了這份謹慎情緒：30年期JGB標售強勁，但漲勢僅短暫維持，財政及BOJ政策正常化疑慮旋即重新主導市場。信用市場融資管道仍然暢通，但初級市場疲乏與科技股領跌，使融資條件變得更加嚴格。

## 美債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

以整體數字來看，美債跌勢異常平均。2年期殖利率上升6個基點至4.19%，5年期上升6個基點至4.27%，10年期上升7個基點至4.55%，30年期則上升6個基點至5.05%。此走勢完全逆轉週一10年期以內券種的漲幅，並使長債在5%以上進一步走弱。以1週變動來看，升幅仍隨到期年限逐步擴大——2年期上升5個基點、5年期8個基點、10年期11個基點、30年期14個基點——延續較廣泛的長存續期間折價。

由於拋售遍及每一個主要到期年限，殖利率曲線斜率的變動小於絕對殖利率。2s10s利差擴大1個基點至36，2s30s則維持於86。5年期表現略優於兩翼，使2s5s10s蝶式利差下降1個基點至-20。2s10s30s蝶式利差上升2個基點至-14，反映指標券區段承受額外壓力。因此，週二是接近平行的熊市走勢，10年期略為落後，而非進入明確的新趨陡格局。

抗通膨債券顯示了主要驅動因素。10年期TIPS實質殖利率上升6個基點至2.30%，相應的損益兩平通膨率則上升1個基點至2.25%。5年期、7年期及20年期實質殖利率分別上升5、6及6個基點，至1.99%、2.14%及2.65%；30年期實質殖利率上升5個基點至2.84%。實質殖利率全面走弱，顯示投資人提高了整個存續期間範圍所要求的報酬，即使油價衝擊再起也僅小幅推高指標通膨補償。

能源是眼前的通膨催化劑。荷莫茲海峽商船再度遭受攻擊，加上美國決定撤銷允許伊朗出售石油的豁免，使WTI期貨盤中曾上漲5.8%。這重新開啟了先前支撐實質殖利率回落的傳導管道：能源壓力緩解曾降低進一步緊縮的急迫性，而新的供給威脅則提高整體通膨降溫停滯，並回頭影響家庭預期或更廣泛定價行為的風險。

6月消費者預期調查使這項回饋風險更為具體。1年期通膨預期由3.5%升至3.7%，3年期指標則由3.1%升至3.3%，創2022年6月以來最高。5年期預期維持在3%。家庭預期汽油通膨下降、食品通膨小幅下降，但醫療照護及租金成本上升，推高近期與中期指標。這種組合顯示，即使週二油價反轉使市場眼前的敏感度升高，能源本身仍不足以解釋預期通膨的黏著性。

紐約Fed總裁John Williams提出制衡觀點，主張能源價格下降應會在未來幾個月壓低整體通膨，且目前政策定位適當。他強調不確定性，加上6月決策移除明確利率指引，使這項判斷仍附帶條件。選擇權市場反映相同張力：交換市場計入年底前約32個基點的緊縮幅度，但投資人也買進可在升息預期消退或政策最終轉向降息時受惠的策略。收盤反映的是偏緊縮的機率分布，而非對單一路徑具有信心。

5月貿易數據提供有利存續期間資產的成長證據，卻未能主導盤勢。商品及服務貿易逆差由修正後的$546億擴大至$776億，出口下降3.2%至$3,177億，進口上升3.3%至$3,953億。實質商品逆差增加18.7%至$1,000億，削弱淨出口對第2季成長追蹤值的貢獻。利率市場中，這項拖累遭能源、通膨預期及供給因素蓋過，顯示市場更重視價格與出清風險傳導，而非單一偏弱的成長數據。

美國財政部3年期標售對供給疑慮形成重要限制。這筆$580億標售的得標殖利率為4.179%，低於投標截止時標售前交易的4.185%。1級交易商獲配7.7%，創該期限歷史新低，顯示殖利率上升後投資人參與強勁。結果證明，新券讓利可在政策敏感端吸引需求，儘管大盤持續走弱，而更大型的存續期間測試在收盤時仍未獲解決。

現金管理券走勢較為分散。4週期的附息券等值殖利率下降2個基點至3.65%，6週期上升5個基點至3.71%，8週期維持在3.72%。13週期殖利率下降1個基點至3.82%，17週期維持在3.87%，26週期上升1個基點至3.96%，52週期則大漲11個基點至4.05%。1年期的劇烈變動，使較長端國庫券區段更貼近市場對2026年政策路徑的重新定價。

當日2場國庫券標售以不同的分配結構獲得承接。364天期國庫券接受投標小計為$520.002564億，最高得標利率3.860%，投標倍數3.14；間接投標人獲配60.13%，直接投標人4.56%，交易商35.31%。42天期發行規模為$900.001893億，最高得標利率3.635%，投標倍數2.74；間接投標人獲配63.51%，直接投標人2.70%，交易商33.79%。兩者皆於7月9日發行及交割，使週二的需求結果與現金移轉分開。

因此，利率收盤同時傳達2項訊息。實質殖利率、油價及家庭通膨預期上升，逆轉先前殖利率曲線中段的反彈，並使年底前進一步緊縮的風險持續存在。3年期投標強勁且國庫券承接有序，則不支持市場全面拒絕政府債券的看法。問題在於市場出清利率門檻升高，集中反映通膨不確定性、反覆供給及存續期間補償；貿易逆差與偏鴿派政策避險部位，則避免市場形成更加單向的鷹派解讀。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

上市信用債隨美債普遍走弱，其中存續期間較長的投資等級債跌幅較大。LQD下跌0.73%，HYG與JNK則分別下跌0.14%及0.10%。此格局符合基礎殖利率上升的影響，並不表示各信用等級惡化程度相同。即使股市走弱，非投資等級債基金表現仍相對穩健，因此初級市場執行情況更能清楚衡量當日的承接壓力。

Amazon以罕見規模提供這項測試。公司發行$250億、共8期的債券，年期介於3年至40年，完成其2026年美元融資需求。籌資所得可能用於一般企業用途，包括償還債務、併購及資本支出。到期年限橫跨殖利率曲線，其中最末1期於2066年到期，因此在美債殖利率原已上升的交易日，這筆交易直接競逐長存續期間的資產負債表承擔能力。

需求仍然可觀，但隨定價收緊而減弱。訂單曾達到$620億，最終利差收窄後降至約$410億，使需求約為交易規模的1.6倍。年內美國投資等級債交易的平均認購倍數約為4倍。最長年期券種的定價約較美債高125個基點，其利差已收窄20個基點；然而，Amazon提供的新券讓利仍是近幾個月常見水準的數倍。

這種組合代表市場已從毫不費力的融資管道，轉向對價格敏感的融資管道。投資人承接整筆交易，但訂單簿縮減及較高的初始補償，顯示市場對另一批大量超大規模雲端服務商長存續期間債券的容忍度有限。交易進行期間，已發行科技債走弱。壓力來自供給飽和與相對價值，而非市場懷疑高評等發行人是否能夠取得融資。

Amazon的融資紀錄使承接問題具有結構性。最新發債使其過去1年債券發行額達到約$1,070億，其中包括3月發行的$370億美元債與€145億歐元債，其後又以瑞士法郎及加拿大元借款。公司今年預期支出接近$2,000億。以所有發行人計算，2026年全球AI相關債券供給達到約$3,350億，是2025年規模的2倍以上；Nvidia與SpaceX則各自在6月籌得$250億。

支撐投資的相同規模，也會造成再融資及績效敏感度。反覆發債提高投資等級債投資組合持有的科技業風險部位，且長到期年限遠超近期基礎設施建置週期。SpaceX第1次發行的債券在定價後早已明顯走弱。週二Amazon的結果顯示，強健的資產負債表可維持市場融資管道，但對於集中度、存續期間，以及資本支出可能超過新基礎設施現金回報的風險，投資人如今要求更明確的補償。

歐洲擔保品政策則增添1種發展較慢的信用分化。ECB已開始對作為擔保品的公司債給予氣候不確定性評分，大型污染企業及缺乏可信轉型計畫的發行人更可能面臨估值折減。由於銀行借款及以公司債作為擔保品的使用量偏低，預期短期影響有限。長期而言，這項制度可能加重脆弱發行人的融資劣勢，但不至於造成廣泛的近期流動性衝擊。

因此，信用市場仍然暢通但選擇性提高。美債殖利率上升壓低總報酬，完成的巨額發債展現可觀的市場容量，而訂單簿則顯示這種容量有其價格。當日情況並不像融資停擺，而是揭露了投資等級科技債市場的緩衝空間正在收窄：異常龐大的資本計畫、反覆供給及長年期債券，要求投資人區分發行人實力與市場能夠順利承接的存續期間規模。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

日本仍是最明顯的國際長端壓力來源，即使其30年期標售表現強勁。該期限第1次發行4%票息債券，投標倍數達4.55，創2019年以來最高，高於前次標售的2.94及過去12個月平均的3.41。殖利率起初曾下降7個基點。但數小時內漲勢便告逆轉，投資人重新聚焦財政前景、日圓疲弱，以及BOJ政策正常化可能落後於通膨進程的疑慮。

因此，標售改善了需求訊號，卻未解決期限溢價問題。偏高的起始殖利率吸引戰術型及終端投資人興趣，但買方組成不足以代表廣泛的長期認可。政府截至2041年3月的¥370兆投資藍圖，以及以債務對GDP比率作為主要財政衡量標準的方針，使發債與名目成長假設維持核心地位。較高的日本國內市場出清利率可能降低日本買方持有海外存續期間資產的相對吸引力，並將壓力傳導至美債。

國內所得與消費強化了BOJ持續推動政策正常化的理由。5月名目現金薪資較1年前成長3.2%，前值修正為成長3.6%，連續第4個月達到或高於3%。底薪增加3%，實質薪資成長1.4%，連續第5個月上升。BOJ實質消費活動指數5月上升0.6%，其中耐久財增加1.2%、非耐久財0.9%、服務0.2%。需求面更為廣泛，使弱勢貨幣造成的通膨問題更難單靠耐心解決。

政府溝通強化了這條政策傳導管道。經濟財政政策大臣Minoru Kiuchi否認政府尋求刻意壓低利率，政府也曾參與將政策利率調升至1%的會議。他的立場使市場更加關注年底前BOJ是否再度行動，同時也使財政公信力成為限制條件：貨幣政策收緊可穩定通膨預期，但擴張性的投資議程仍要求債券市場承接龐大的長期融資負擔。

歐洲帶來溫和的上行壓力，但未形成一致的鷹派訊號。德國5月工業生產上升0.9%，其中汽車產出增加3.6%並居主導地位；年產量持平。資本財與消費財產出上升，中間財則下降0.4%。數據改善近期活動前景，但尚不足以代表全面加速，在能源不確定性持續之際，為德國公債殖利率提供更堅挺的成長訊號。

ECB管理委員會成員Fabio Panetta形容前景脆弱，通膨面臨上行風險的同時，成長也面臨下行風險。他為ECB在6月升息後避免預設政策路徑辯護，並主張目前的供給衝擊與2022年不同。這使歐洲利率形成雙向傳導：能源干擾再起可能支撐通膨補償，成長偏弱則限制了連續自動緊縮的理由。

義大利重返美元主權債市場，顯示差異化的歐洲財政風險仍能吸引外資。財政部透過5年期、10年期及30年期指標債籌得$60億，訂單接近$200億，需求集中於較短年期。義大利10年期債券相對德國的利差，已由Giorgia Meloni上任時約250個基點收窄至約77個基點。強勁的市場融資能力降低眼前再融資疑慮，但2026年規劃中的中長期發債規模達€3,500億至€3,650億，供給依然可觀。

中國擴大跨境人民幣管道，成為另一種存續期間傳導形式。香港人民幣業務資金安排將增加逾1倍至人民幣5,000億元，債券通「南向通」年度額度則由5,000億提高至人民幣8,000億元。更多離岸人民幣流動性，以及中國大陸機構購買離岸債券的更大空間，可拓展區域融資與投資人資金流。這無法消除日本或美國的主權期限溢價壓力，但可擴充另一個貨幣流動性及債券需求來源。

因此，全球存續期間環境並不像單看美國收盤所顯示的那樣一致不利。日本與義大利標售強勁，證明適當的殖利率及架構能吸引買方；德國產出改善，中國也擴大市場容量。然而，日本標售後走勢持續反轉、龐大財政規模及BOJ政策正常化風險，使跨境長端門檻維持不變。週二的美債拋售延伸了這種全球分化：市場融資管道依然存在，但在通膨、財政供給與買方誘因交會的市場中，持有存續期間需要更高補償。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

隨著美債殖利率與美元同步上升，新興市場美元債走弱。EMB下跌0.43%，美元指數上升0.29%至101.14。這種組合提高硬通貨借款人共同面臨的折現率與匯率門檻，但整體變動無法描述每一個主權國家的利差表現。油價則進一步區分出口國與進口國，尤其是當日反彈中斷了幅度大得多的月度跌勢。

巴西呈現最急迫的債務管理案例。在規模2.3兆-雷亞爾的抗通膨債市場中，財政疑慮與需求變化已將實質殖利率推近8%。財政部先前已縮小標售規模並取消發行以維持流動性，官員也表示，若這些措施無法使市場條件恢復平衡，仍可進行買回。運用現金及調整發債可減少近期市場失序，但也顯示公共支出增加正使通膨控制及未來政策寬鬆預期變得更加複雜。

中國的外部緩衝仍然龐大，但有所下降。6月官方外匯存底減少$260億，或0.75%，至$3.4163兆。主管機關將變動歸因於美元走強下的匯率換算及全球資產價格漲跌互見。評價因素限制了將外匯存底下降直接解讀為干預或資本外逃的合理性。即便如此，這項下降仍凸顯區域資產負債表對美元波動的敏感性，而擴大的香港人民幣資金安排則提供另一條本幣流動性管道。

菲律賓說明了整體通膨下降為何不會自動緩解當地政策風險。6月通膨由6.8%降至6.4%，略低於所引述的6.5%預估中位數，但仍超過央行3%目標的2倍。核心通膨加速至4.4%，創2023年12月以來最高，促使菲律賓央行保留進一步行動的選項。數據公布後披索小幅升值，反映市場對整體數字的寬慰，但黏著的基礎壓力使當地利率維持緊縮。

因此，各金融工具與主權國家的債務情勢有所分化。巴西的問題集中於本幣抗通膨債的存續期間及財政公信力；中國的外匯存底變動涉及評價與外部緩衝的觀感；菲律賓則面對國內通膨及政策傳導問題。美國實質殖利率與美元上升，使共同背景收緊，而各國的現金管理、外匯存底能力及央行公信力，則決定這項衝擊有多少會傳導至再融資成本。

## 跨資產

跨資產走勢強化通膨型避險的解讀，而非資金轉入政府債券尋求安全。隨著荷莫茲海峽航運威脅再起，WTI上漲2.76%至$70.44，Brent上漲3.01%至$74.16。美元上升0.29%，美債則遭拋售。因此，在能源衝擊推高政策維持緊縮的預期及實質殖利率之際，避險需求青睞美元，使名目債券同時暴露於通膨不確定性及更高折現率。

股市弱勢集中於科技業。S&P 500下跌0.45%，Nasdaq 100下跌1.77%，費城半導體指數重挫4.65%。市場擔憂大規模AI投資可能不足以支撐偏高的估值，使晶片製造商承壓；與此同時，Amazon融資案則凸顯基礎設施支出的規模。兩者透過相同的資本支出主題連結股市重新定價與信用債供給，但整體指數跌幅溫和，顯示壓力尚未演變成全經濟範圍的清算。

波動率上升3.60%，但收於16.13；相較半導體類股的跌幅，仍屬受控水準。非投資等級債基金跌幅也僅屬溫和。這種組合顯示，產業集中度及通膨不確定性是主要風險傳導管道。其並不支持會使美債殖利率通常下跌的深度成長衝擊，也解釋了為何貿易對成長的貢獻轉弱，卻未成為當日跨資產核心敘事。

儘管地緣政治緊張再起，黃金仍下跌0.24%至$4,145.30。實質殖利率上升與美元走強，提高無息資產在持有成本方面的劣勢，壓過眼前的避險需求。黃金未能上漲，與美債拋售及美元走強相互呼應。即使黃金在上月進入熊市後仍維持於$4,000以上，這波走勢也延續與高殖利率相關的廣泛壓力。

不同時間範圍下的能源訊號仍不明朗。儘管週二上漲，WTI與Brent在1個月期間仍分別下跌22.20%及20.34%。單日衝擊重新打開通膨尾部風險，而月度跌幅則仍可能出現以下情況：供給條件與需求最終仍會使能源價格低於先前高點。因此，跨資產走勢支持週二對實質殖利率及通膨風險的重新定價，但尚未證明通膨降溫趨勢已永久逆轉。

## 市場前瞻意義

7月8日下午2:00 ET公布的6月16–17日FOMC會議紀要，其後7月9日上午8:30公布的初領及續領失業救濟金人數，以及7月14日上午8:30公布的6月CPI，共同構成政策路徑測試。若委員會廣泛支持2026年再度升息、勞動市場流量維持韌性，或整體、核心及住房通膨堅挺，2年至5年期區段將保持敏感，並驗證週二實質殖利率及家庭通膨疑慮上升的走勢。若委員會立場分歧、申領數據轉弱且通膨細節降溫，則將支持選擇權市場對激進緊縮的避險部位，挑戰週一殖利率曲線中段反彈遭逆轉的走勢，並區分暫時性石油衝擊與政策反應函數的持久轉變。

指標券供給移向長端：7月8日下午1:00 ET將增額發行$390億、票面利率4.375%的10年期公債；7月9日下午1:00則將增額發行$220億、票面利率5.000%的30年期公債；兩者均於7月15日交割。相對同期發行前交易殖利率的得標結果、投標倍數、直接與間接投標人獲配、交易商留存，以及次級市場表現，都是衡量存續期間承擔能力的可觀察指標。終端投資人投標強勁且標售後交易穩定，將部分化解Amazon巨額發債及長債殖利率突破5%所引發的疑慮。若參與疲弱或持續走弱，則將確認實質利率及供給補償正在Fed下次決策之外，持續推高長端門檻。

全球通膨與存續期間傳導管道，取決於7月7日晚上10:00 ET的紐西蘭央行決策、7月8日晚上9:30 ET公布的中國6月消費者及生產者物價，以及7月9日東京交易時段公布的日本區域經濟評估與規模約¥2.5兆的5年期JGB標售。若紐西蘭央行保持耐心、中國物價偏軟，且日本區域評估均衡並伴隨強勁標售需求，將挑戰跨市場期限溢價壓力持續存在的看法。若紐西蘭訊息轉緊、中國商品通膨轉強，或日本薪資與物價壓力擴大並伴隨JGB投標疲弱，則將確認政策分歧；即使美國國內數據轉弱，美國指標券及長端殖利率仍將面臨上行風險。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為基準。

overall_summary.headline: 能源、實質利率與供給重新定價，帶動美債全面拋售
overall_summary.session_narrative: 截至完整紐約收盤，殖利率已沿整條殖利率曲線上升，主要由實質利率走弱所帶動，油價風險及更堅挺的家庭通膨預期則重新引發政策不確定性。美國公債與公司債供給放大重新定價幅度，科技股領跌也強化了資本支出傳導管道。3年期美債需求強勁、損益兩平通膨率受控且非投資等級債表現穩健，限制了市場將此解讀為全面性融資或成長衝擊的空間。
overall_summary.summary: 隨著能源風險再起、實質殖利率上升及大量發債提高市場出清利率門檻，美債逆轉週一由殖利率曲線中段領漲的反彈。短端標售需求強勁、損益兩平通膨率受控，使走勢維持有序；信用市場融資管道仍然暢通，但條件轉趨嚴格。

section_summaries.market_read: 這波廣泛的存續期間資產拋售由實質利率重新定價主導，而非通膨預期失控。
section_summaries.next_check: 長端標售需求將顯示，較高殖利率能否恢復持久的承接力道。
section_summaries.summary: 週二美債拋售在前一交易日殖利率曲線中段反彈後，重新反映全面性的通膨與供給折價。能源風險再起、大量主權債與公司債發行，以及更高的實質利率門檻，推動殖利率曲線走弱；短端標售需求穩健且損益兩平通膨率受控，則限制了失序風險。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤反映更高的市場出清利率門檻，但未顯示全面性需求失靈。
section_summaries.next_check: CPI、勞動市場申領數據與政策細節，必須確認殖利率曲線中段的弱勢能否持續。
section_summaries.section_title: 美債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 利率近乎平行上升，指標券略為落後，長存續期間資產的折價則持續存在。實質殖利率全面走弱、家庭通膨預期轉強、油價風險及供給壓力，蓋過較疲弱的貿易數據；不過，3年期標售投標強勁，顯示新券讓利仍能吸引投資人。

section_summaries.market_read: 初級市場執行情況反映供給疲乏，而非全面性融資停擺。
section_summaries.next_check: 次級市場表現及下一筆大型交易，將檢驗投資人剩餘的存續期間承擔能力。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 信用市場融資管道維持暢通，但隨著美債殖利率上升打擊存續期間較長的投資等級債，市場對價格變得更加敏感。Amazon完成巨額發債，但縮減的訂單簿與偏高的新券讓利，凸顯市場承接反覆、大量且長天期科技債供給的緩衝空間正在縮小。

section_summaries.market_read: 全球需求依然存在，但財政與政策分歧使長端持續面臨風險。
section_summaries.next_check: 日本5年期標售與區域經濟評估，將檢驗買方支撐能否持續。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 日本與義大利標售強勁，顯示適當的殖利率及架構仍能吸引買盤，但日本標售後的走勢反轉，使全球期限溢價警訊持續存在。BOJ政策正常化、財政規模、能源不確定性及歐洲成長不均，令跨境持有存續期間所需的補償維持高檔。

section_summaries.market_read: 共同的外部門檻升高，而各國特有的緩衝能力決定再融資壓力。
section_summaries.next_check: 美元走向及巴西對標售的調整，將顯示流動性壓力集中於何處。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 新興市場債務面臨美元走強與美國實質殖利率上升，但各主權國家與金融工具受到的傳導並不相同。巴西的本幣債務管理、中國的外匯存底評價效應，以及菲律賓持續偏高的核心通膨，使財政能力與政策公信力成為關鍵。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產走勢強化較高實質利率體制，而非有利存續期間資產的成長衝擊。
section_summaries.next_check: 能源漲勢的持續性與股市參與範圍是否擴大，將顯示衝擊是否正在蔓延。
section_summaries.summary: 油價、美元及實質殖利率上升，科技股、美債與黃金則走弱，形成通膨型避險組合。波動率受控且非投資等級債表現穩健，顯示壓力集中於科技業與通膨，而非整體經濟全面清算。

section_summaries.market_read: 未來1週將區分這是暫時性的石油與供給衝擊，還是持久的利率重設。
section_summaries.next_check: 依照各項既定觸發條件，觀察政策數據、標售內部指標及全球價格訊號。
section_summaries.summary: 目前有3項測試主導核心論點：國內政策與通膨數據、美債長端供給，以及全球通膨與存續期間傳導管道。其結果將決定週二的實質利率重新定價會持續、隨承接改善而緩和，或因政策分歧而擴大。

watch_events.market_implication: 結果若偏強，2年至5年期殖利率將持續面臨上行風險；細節若偏軟，寬鬆避險部位將再度活躍。
watch_events.market_trigger: 觸發條件是廣泛支持升息、申領數據維持韌性，或核心與住房通膨堅挺。
watch_events.title: 美國政策路徑測試
watch_events.watch_line: 會議紀要、申領失業救濟金數據及CPI，將檢驗通膨黏著性是否使政策維持緊縮。

watch_events.market_implication: 承接壓力將使長端實質利率與供給溢價延續至Fed下次決策之後。
watch_events.market_trigger: 參與疲弱或標售後持續走弱，將確認市場承接壓力。
watch_events.title: 美債長端供給測試
watch_events.watch_line: 得標殖利率、投標人獲配、交易商留存及次級市場交易將衡量存續期間承擔能力。

watch_events.market_implication: 即使美國國內數據轉弱，這種組合仍將使美國指標券及長端殖利率面臨上行風險。
watch_events.market_trigger: 紐西蘭央行立場轉緊、中國物價轉強，或更廣泛薪資壓力伴隨JGB需求疲弱。
watch_events.title: 全球通膨與存續期間測試
watch_events.watch_line: 紐西蘭央行指引、中國物價，以及日本的經濟評估與標售，將檢驗跨市場壓力。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The July 7 Treasury close reversed the previous session’s belly-led relief and restored a broad inflation-and-supply discount across the curve. The 10-year yield finished at 4.55% and the 30-year at 5.05%, with maturities from 2 through 30 years cheapening by roughly 6 to 7 basis points. The move was neither a simple growth upgrade nor a classic haven response to geopolitical stress. It combined a renewed energy shock, heavy public and corporate issuance, and a higher real-rate hurdle into a synchronized selloff.

This changed the near-term interpretation carried from July 6. Cooling services activity and logistics pressure had allowed 5- and 10-year real yields to ease, even as the long bond remained constrained by term premium. Tuesday erased that intermediate-maturity reprieve. Renewed attacks around the Strait of Hormuz lifted oil and revived concern that lower energy inflation could prove temporary. WTI ended at $70.44, and the inflation impulse arrived alongside a New York Fed survey showing higher 1- and 3-year household price expectations.

Supply amplified the macro repricing. Amazon’s unexpected jumbo transaction placed long-dated corporate duration into a market already preparing for Treasury coupon auctions. The company completed the deal, yet a sharply reduced orderbook and elevated concession showed that even top-tier technology borrowers face a finite absorption pool. Strong bidding at the 3-year Treasury auction limited the case for a generalized demand failure, but it did not prevent benchmark and long yields from breaking through levels that had anchored the previous close.

The composition of the move gives real rates the leading role. The 10-year real yield rose 6 basis points, compared with a 1-basis-point rise in breakeven inflation, so the oil shock did not translate into an uncontrolled inflation-expectations move. Investors instead required more real compensation for holding duration through an uncertain Fed path, large issuance and cross-market pressure. Japan reinforced that caution: a strong 30-year JGB auction generated only a brief rally as fiscal and BOJ-normalization concerns reasserted themselves. Credit access remained open, but primary-market fatigue and a technology-led equity selloff made that access more conditional.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury losses were unusually even in headline terms. The 2-year yield rose 6 basis points to 4.19%, the 5-year added 6 to 4.27%, the 10-year climbed 7 to 4.55%, and the 30-year increased 6 to 5.05%. This reversed all of Monday’s rally through 10 years and extended the long bond’s weakness above 5%. Weekly changes still became progressively larger with maturity—5 basis points at 2 years, eight at five, 11 at 10 and 14 at 30—preserving the broader long-duration penalty.

Curve slopes moved less than outright yields because the selloff reached every major maturity. The 2s10s spread widened 1 basis point to 36, while 2s30s held at 86. The 2s5s10s butterfly fell 1 basis point to -20 as the 5-year performed slightly better than its wings. The 2s10s30s butterfly rose 2 basis points to -14, capturing the extra pressure around the benchmark sector. Tuesday was therefore a near-parallel bear move with modest 10-year underperformance, not a decisive new steepening regime.

Inflation-linked securities identify the dominant component. The 10-year TIPS real yield rose 6 basis points to 2.30%, while the corresponding breakeven increased 1 basis point to 2.25%. 5-, 7- and 20-year real yields rose 5, 6 and 6 basis points to 1.99%, 2.14% and 2.65%; the 30-year real yield added 5 to 2.84%. The uniform real-yield cheapening shows that investors raised the return demanded across the duration spectrum, even though the renewed oil shock nudged benchmark inflation compensation higher.

Energy supplied the immediate inflation catalyst. Renewed attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz and the US decision to revoke a waiver permitting Iranian oil sales pushed WTI futures as much as 5.8% higher intraday. That reopened a channel which had supported the prior session’s real-yield relief: energy relief had reduced the urgency of additional tightening, whereas a fresh supply threat raised the risk that headline disinflation would stall and feed back into household expectations or broader pricing behavior.

The June Survey of Consumer Expectations made that feedback risk more concrete. 1-year inflation expectations rose to 3.7% from 3.5%, and the 3-year measure increased to 3.3% from 3.1%, its highest since June 2022. 5-year expectations held at 3%. Households anticipated less gasoline inflation and slightly less food inflation, but higher medical-care and rent costs lifted the near- and medium-term measures. The mix suggests that energy alone does not explain the persistence of expected inflation, even as Tuesday’s oil reversal sharpened the immediate market sensitivity.

New York Fed President John Williams supplied a counterweight by arguing that falling energy prices should lower overall inflation in coming months and that current policy was well positioned. His emphasis on uncertainty and the June decision to remove explicit rate guidance left that assessment conditional. Options markets reflected the same tension: swaps carried about 32 basis points of tightening through year-end, yet investors were also buying structures that benefited if hike expectations faded or policy ultimately shifted toward cuts. The close priced a restrictive distribution, not confidence in one predetermined path.

The May trade release offered duration-friendly growth evidence that failed to dominate the tape. The goods-and-services deficit widened to $77.6 billion from a revised $54.6 billion as exports fell 3.2% to $317.7 billion and imports rose 3.3% to $395.3 billion. The real-goods deficit increased 18.7% to $100.0 billion, weakening the net-export contribution to Q2 growth tracking. That drag was outweighed in rates by energy, inflation expectations and supply, illustrating how the market prioritized price and clearing-risk channels over a single softer growth input.

Treasury’s 3-year sale provided an important limit to the supply concern. The $58 billion auction stopped at 4.179%, below the 4.185% yield in pre-auction trading at the bidding deadline. Primary dealers received 7.7%, a record low for the tenor, indicating strong investor participation after the rise in yields. The result showed that concession could draw demand at the policy-sensitive end, even though the broader market continued to cheapen and the larger duration tests remained unresolved at the close.

Cash bills were more dispersed. The 4-week coupon-equivalent yield fell 2 basis points to 3.65%, the 6-week rose 5 to 3.71%, and the 8-week held at 3.72%. 13-week yields slipped 1 basis point to 3.82%, while 17-week yields were unchanged at 3.87%, 26-week yields rose 1 to 3.96%, and 52-week yields jumped 11 to 4.05%. The sharp 1-year move aligned the outer bill sector more closely with the repricing of the 2026 policy path.

The day’s 2 bill auctions were absorbed with different allocation profiles. The $52.0002564 billion accepted subtotal of 364-day bills stopped at a 3.860% high rate with 3.14 bid-to-cover; indirect bidders took 60.13%, directs 4.56% and dealers 35.31%. The $90.0001893 billion 42-day sale stopped at 3.635% with 2.74 coverage; indirects received 63.51%, directs 2.70% and dealers 33.79%. Both issue and settle July 9, separating Tuesday’s demand outcome from the cash transfer.

The rates close consequently carried two messages at once. Higher real yields, oil and household inflation expectations reversed the prior belly rally and kept year-end tightening risk alive. Strong 3-year bidding and orderly bill absorption argued against a market-wide refusal of government paper. The pressure was a higher clearing-rate problem concentrated around uncertain inflation, repeated supply and duration compensation, with the trade deficit and dovish policy hedges preventing an even more one-sided hawkish reading.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Broad listed credit weakened alongside Treasuries, with the duration-heavy investment-grade segment taking the larger loss. LQD fell 0.73%, compared with declines of 0.14% in HYG and 0.10% in JNK. The pattern fits the rise in underlying yields and does not indicate equal deterioration across quality tiers. High-yield funds proved comparatively resilient even as equities softened, leaving primary-market execution as the clearer measure of same-day absorption stress.

Amazon supplied that test on an exceptional scale. The company sold $25 billion across 8 tranches ranging from 3 to 40 years, completing its US dollar funding needs for 2026. Proceeds may support general corporate purposes, including debt repayment, acquisitions and capital expenditure. The maturity range placed supply across the curve, including the 2066 final tranche, so the transaction competed directly for long-duration balance-sheet capacity on a day when Treasury yields were already rising.

Demand remained substantial but weakened as pricing tightened. Orders peaked at $62 billion and fell to about $41 billion after final spreads were reduced, leaving demand at roughly 1.6 times the deal size. US high-grade transactions had averaged about 4 times coverage during the year. The longest tranche priced around 125 basis points over Treasuries after its spread narrowed by 20 basis points, yet Amazon still offered concessions several times larger than those commonly seen in recent months.

That combination marks a shift from effortless access to price-sensitive access. Investors absorbed the full transaction, but the reduced book and wider starting compensation showed limited tolerance for another large block of hyperscaler duration. Outstanding technology bonds weakened as the deal progressed. The pressure came from supply saturation and relative value, not from doubts about whether a highly rated issuer could obtain funding at all.

Amazon’s financing history makes the absorption issue structural. The latest sale brought its bond issuance over the prior year to about $107 billion, including $37 billion in dollars and €14.5 billion in euros in March, followed by Swiss-franc and Canadian-dollar borrowing. Its expected spending is almost $200 billion this year. Across issuers, AI-linked bond supply reached about $335 billion globally in 2026, more than twice the 2025 volume, while Nvidia and SpaceX each raised $25 billion in June.

The same scale that supports investment also creates refinancing and performance sensitivity. Repeated issuance raises the amount of technology risk held by high-grade portfolios, and long maturities extend well beyond the near-term buildout cycle. SpaceX’s debut bonds had already weakened materially after pricing. Tuesday’s Amazon result showed that strong balance sheets preserve market access, yet investors now demand clearer compensation for concentration, duration and the possibility that capital spending outruns the cash returns generated by new infrastructure.

European collateral policy added a slower-moving form of credit differentiation. The ECB has begun assigning climate uncertainty scores to corporate bonds used as collateral, with larger polluters and issuers lacking credible transition plans more likely to face valuation haircuts. The immediate effect was expected to be limited because bank borrowing and use of corporate bonds as collateral were low. Over time, the framework can raise the funding disadvantage for vulnerable issuers without creating a broad near-term liquidity shock.

Credit therefore remained open and selective. Higher Treasury yields lowered total returns, a completed jumbo sale demonstrated substantial capacity, and the orderbook showed that capacity had a price. The day did not resemble a funding shutdown. It exposed a narrowing cushion in high-grade technology, where unusually large capital programs, repeated supply and long bond maturities require investors to distinguish issuer strength from the amount of duration the market can absorb smoothly.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Japan remained the clearest international source of long-end pressure, even though its 30-year auction was strong. The first 4% coupon on the tenor drew a 4.55 bid-to-cover ratio, the highest since 2019, up from 2.94 at the prior sale and above the 3.41 12-month average. The yield initially fell as much as 7 basis points. That rally reversed within hours as investors returned to the fiscal outlook, a weak yen and concern that BOJ normalization could lag the inflation process.

The auction therefore improved the demand signal without resolving the term-premium problem. High starting yields attracted tactical and end-investor interest, but the buyer breakdown did not amount to a broad long-term endorsement. The government’s ¥370 trillion investment roadmap through March 2041 and its emphasis on debt-to-GDP as the main fiscal yardstick keep issuance and nominal-growth assumptions central. A higher domestic clearing rate can reduce the relative appeal of overseas duration to Japanese buyers, transmitting pressure into Treasuries.

Domestic income and consumption strengthened the case for continued BOJ normalization. Nominal cash earnings rose 3.2% from a year earlier in May after a revised 3.6% gain, marking a 4th month at or above 3%. Base pay increased 3%, and real earnings rose 1.4% for a 5th consecutive gain. The BOJ’s real Consumption Activity Index advanced 0.6% in May, with durable goods up 1.2%, non-durables 0.9% and services 0.2%. Broader demand makes a weak-currency inflation problem harder to address through patience alone.

Government communication reinforced that policy channel. Growth Strategy Minister Minoru Kiuchi rejected the view that the administration sought artificially low rates, and the government had participated in the meeting that raised the policy rate to 1%. His stance increased attention on another BOJ move before year-end. It also left fiscal credibility as the limiting condition: tighter monetary policy can anchor inflation expectations, but an expansive investment agenda still requires the bond market to accept a large long-run funding burden.

Europe added moderate upward pressure without a uniform hawkish signal. German industrial production rose 0.9% in May, led by a 3.6% increase in automotive output; annual production was flat. Capital-goods and consumer-goods output advanced, whereas intermediate goods fell 0.4%. The release improved the near-term activity picture but stopped short of a broad acceleration, giving Bund yields a firmer growth input alongside persistent energy uncertainty.

ECB Governing Council member Fabio Panetta described the outlook as fragile, with upside inflation risks coexisting with downside growth risks. He defended avoiding a predetermined policy path after the ECB’s June rate increase and argued that the current supply shock differed from 2022. The result is a two-sided European rates channel: renewed energy disruption can sustain inflation compensation, while softer growth limits the case for an automatic sequence of tightening.

Italy’s return to the dollar sovereign market showed that differentiated European fiscal risk could still attract foreign capital. The Treasury raised $6 billion through 5-, 10- and 30-year benchmarks and drew almost $20 billion of orders, with demand concentrated in the shorter tranches. Italy’s 10-year spread over Germany had narrowed to about 77 basis points from roughly 250 when Giorgia Meloni took office. Strong access reduced immediate refinancing concern, though planned 2026 medium- and long-term issuance of €350 billion to €365 billion keeps supply material.

China widened cross-border renminbi channels as another form of duration transmission. The RMB Business Facility in Hong Kong will more than double to 500 billion yuan, and the annual Southbound Bond Connect quota will rise to 800 billion yuan from 500 billion. Greater offshore yuan liquidity and more capacity for mainland institutions to buy offshore bonds can broaden regional funding and investor flows. It does not remove sovereign term-premium pressure in Japan or the US, but it expands an alternative pool of currency liquidity and bond demand.

Global duration was thus less uniformly adverse than the US close alone suggested. Strong Japanese and Italian auctions demonstrated that yield and structure could attract buyers, while German output improved and China expanded market capacity. The persistence of Japan’s post-auction reversal, fiscal scale and BOJ normalization risk kept the cross-border long-end hurdle intact. Tuesday’s Treasury selloff extended that global dispersion: access remained available, but duration needed higher compensation in markets where inflation, fiscal supply and buyer incentives intersected.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market dollar debt weakened as both Treasury yields and the dollar rose. EMB fell 0.43%, while the US Dollar Index gained 0.29% to 101.14. The combination raised the common discount-rate and currency hurdle for hard-currency borrowers, though the aggregate move did not describe every sovereign’s spread behavior. Oil added another division between exporters and importers, especially because the daily rebound interrupted a much larger monthly decline.

Brazil presented the most acute debt-management case. Fiscal concern and shifting demand had pushed real yields near 8% in a 2.3 trillion-real inflation-linked market. The Treasury had already reduced auction sizes and canceled sales to preserve liquidity, and officials said buybacks remained available if those steps failed to rebalance conditions. The ability to use cash and alter issuance can reduce near-term dislocation, yet it also reveals how higher public spending is complicating inflation control and expectations for future policy easing.

China’s external buffer remained large but moved lower. Official reserves declined $26.0 billion, or 0.75%, in June to $3.4163 trillion. Authorities attributed the change to exchange-rate translation and mixed global asset-price moves as the dollar strengthened. The valuation explanation limits a direct reading of reserve loss as intervention or capital flight. Even so, the decline highlights the sensitivity of regional balance sheets to dollar moves, while the enlarged Hong Kong yuan facilities provide a separate domestic-currency liquidity channel.

The Philippines illustrated why lower headline inflation does not automatically ease local policy risk. June inflation slowed to 6.4% from 6.8%, slightly below the cited 6.5% median estimate, but remained more than twice the central bank’s 3% target. Core inflation accelerated to 4.4%, its highest since December 2023, prompting the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas to preserve the option of further action. A modest peso gain after the release reflected relief on the headline, while sticky underlying pressure kept local rates restrictive.

Debt conditions were consequently split by instrument and sovereign. Brazil’s problem centered on local inflation-linked duration and fiscal credibility; China’s reserve change concerned valuation and external-buffer optics; the Philippines faced domestic inflation and policy transmission. The rise in US real yields and the dollar tightened the shared backdrop, while country-specific cash management, reserve capacity and central-bank credibility determined how much of that shock reached refinancing costs.

## Cross asset

Cross assets reinforced an inflationary risk-off interpretation, not a flight into government duration. WTI rose 2.76% to $70.44 and Brent gained 3.01% to $74.16 as threats to Strait of Hormuz traffic returned. The dollar advanced 0.29%, while Treasuries sold off. Haven demand therefore favored the currency as the energy shock lifted expected policy restraint and real yields, leaving nominal bonds exposed to both inflation uncertainty and a higher discount rate.

Equity weakness was concentrated in technology. The S&P 500 fell 0.45%, the Nasdaq 100 lost 1.77%, and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index dropped 4.65%. Concern that large AI investments may not justify elevated valuations hit chipmakers at the same time Amazon’s financing highlighted the scale of infrastructure spending. The overlap linked equity repricing and credit supply through one capital-expenditure theme, although the modest broad-index decline showed that the stress had not become an economy-wide liquidation.

Volatility rose 3.60% but ended at 16.13, a contained level compared with the scale of semiconductor losses. High-yield funds also declined only modestly. That combination points to sector concentration and inflation uncertainty as the dominant risk channels. It offers little support for a deep growth shock that would normally pull Treasury yields lower, and it explains why the weaker trade contribution did not become the day’s central cross-asset narrative.

Gold slipped 0.24% to $4,145.30 despite renewed geopolitical tension. Higher real yields and a stronger dollar raised the carrying-cost disadvantage of a non-yielding asset, overwhelming the immediate haven impulse. Its failure to rally aligned with the Treasury selloff and dollar strength. The move also continued the broader pressure associated with elevated yields, even though gold remained above $4,000 after the previous month’s bear-market break.

The energy signal remained unsettled across horizons. WTI and Brent were still down 22.20% and 20.34% over 1 month despite Tuesday’s rise. The daily shock reopened the inflation tail, while the monthly declines preserved the possibility that supply conditions and demand ultimately keep energy below earlier peaks. Cross assets therefore supported Tuesday’s real-yield and inflation-risk repricing without establishing that the disinflation trend had permanently reversed.

## Forward Market Significance

The June 16–17 FOMC minutes at 2:00 p.m. ET on July 8, followed by initial and continuing claims at 8:30 a.m. on July 9 and June CPI at 8:30 a.m. on July 14, define the policy-path test. Broad committee support for another 2026 increase, resilient labor flows or firm headline, core and shelter inflation would keep the 2- to 5-year sector sensitive and validate Tuesday’s rise in real yields and household inflation concern. A divided committee, softer claims and cooler inflation detail would support the options-market hedge against aggressive tightening, challenge the reversal of Monday’s belly rally, and separate a temporary oil shock from a persistent shift in the reaction function.

Benchmark supply moves to the long end with the $39 billion reopening of the 4.375% 10-year note at 1:00 p.m. ET on July 8 and the $22 billion reopening of the 5.000% 30-year bond at 1:00 p.m. on July 9; both settle July 15. Stops against contemporaneous when-issued yields, bid-to-cover, direct and indirect awards, dealer retention and secondary performance are the observable measures of duration capacity. Strong end-investor bidding and stable post-sale trading would resolve part of the concern created by Amazon’s jumbo transaction and the move through 5% in the long bond. Weak participation or continued cheapening would confirm that real-rate and supply compensation, beyond the next Fed decision, is sustaining the long-end hurdle.

The global inflation-and-duration channel turns on the RBNZ decision at 10:00 p.m. ET on July 7, China’s June consumer and producer prices at 9:30 p.m. ET on July 8, and Japan’s regional economic assessment and roughly ¥2.5 trillion 5-year JGB auction during Tokyo trading on July 9. A patient RBNZ, softer Chinese prices and balanced Japanese regional assessments accompanied by strong auction demand would challenge the persistence of cross-market term-premium pressure. A tighter New Zealand message, firmer Chinese goods inflation, or broader Japanese wage-and-price strength with weak JGB bidding would confirm policy divergence and keep US benchmark and long yields exposed even if domestic data soften.

section_summaries.market_read: Real-rate repricing, not runaway inflation expectations, led the broad duration selloff.
section_summaries.next_check: Long-end auction demand will show whether higher yields can restore durable absorption.
section_summaries.summary: Tuesday’s Treasury selloff restored a broad inflation-and-supply discount after the prior belly rally. Renewed energy risk, heavy sovereign and corporate issuance, and a higher real-rate hurdle cheapened the curve, while solid short-end auction demand and contained breakevens limited the case for disorder.

section_summaries.market_read: The close priced a higher clearing-rate hurdle without signaling a broad demand failure.
section_summaries.next_check: CPI, labor claims, and policy detail must confirm whether belly weakness can persist.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Rates rose almost in parallel, with modest benchmark underperformance and a persistent long-duration penalty. Uniform real-yield cheapening, firmer household inflation expectations, oil risk, and supply outweighed softer trade data, though strong 3-year bidding showed that concession still attracted investors.

section_summaries.market_read: Primary execution points to supply fatigue rather than a generalized funding shutdown.
section_summaries.next_check: Secondary performance and the next large deal will test investors’ remaining duration capacity.
section_summaries.summary: Credit access stayed open but became more price-sensitive as higher Treasury yields hit duration-heavy investment grade. Amazon completed its jumbo offering, yet the reduced orderbook and elevated concession exposed a narrowing absorption cushion for repeated, long-dated technology issuance.

section_summaries.market_read: Global demand remains available, but fiscal and policy divergence keeps the long end exposed.
section_summaries.next_check: Japan’s 5-year auction and regional assessment will test whether buyer support can endure.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Strong Japanese and Italian auctions showed that yield and structure could still attract buyers, but Japan’s post-auction reversal preserved the global term-premium warning. BOJ normalization, fiscal scale, energy uncertainty, and uneven European growth kept cross-border duration compensation elevated.

section_summaries.market_read: The common external hurdle rose, while country-specific buffers determined refinancing stress.
section_summaries.next_check: Dollar direction and Brazil’s auction adjustments will reveal where liquidity pressure concentrates.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: Emerging-market debt faced a firmer dollar and higher US real yields, but transmission differed by sovereign and instrument. Brazil’s local debt management, China’s reserve valuation effects, and persistent Philippine core inflation made fiscal capacity and policy credibility decisive.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross assets reinforced a higher real-rate regime, not a growth shock that supports duration.
section_summaries.next_check: Energy persistence and broader equity participation will show whether the shock is spreading.
section_summaries.summary: Oil, the dollar, and real yields rose while technology shares, Treasuries, and gold weakened, producing an inflationary risk-off mix. Contained volatility and resilient high yield indicated concentrated technology and inflation stress rather than an economy-wide liquidation.

section_summaries.market_read: The next week separates a temporary oil-and-supply shock from a durable rate reset.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch policy data, auction internals, and global price signals against each stated trigger.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests now govern the thesis: domestic policy and inflation data, long-end Treasury supply, and the global inflation-duration channel. Their outcomes will determine whether Tuesday’s real-rate repricing persists, eases with better absorption, or broadens through policy divergence.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.3% (+6 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.25% (+1 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 36 bp (+1 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 86 bp (-0 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Treasuries sell off broadly as energy, real rates, and supply reset the close
overall_summary.session_narrative: At the completed NY close, yields had risen across the curve, with real-rate cheapening doing most of the work as oil risk and firmer household inflation expectations revived policy uncertainty. Treasury and corporate supply amplified the repricing, while technology-led equity weakness reinforced the capital-spending channel. Strong 3-year demand, contained breakevens, and resilient high yield limited the case for a generalized funding or growth shock.
overall_summary.summary: Treasuries reversed Monday’s belly-led relief as renewed energy risk, higher real yields, and heavy issuance raised the clearing-rate hurdle. Strong short-end auction demand and contained breakevens kept the move orderly, while credit access remained open but more conditional.

watch_events.market_implication: A firm outcome would keep 2- to 5-year yields exposed; softer detail would revive easing hedges.
watch_events.market_trigger: The trigger is broad hike support, resilient claims, or firm core and shelter inflation.
watch_events.title: US policy-path test
watch_events.watch_line: Minutes, claims, and CPI will test whether inflation persistence keeps policy restrictive.

watch_events.market_implication: Strain would sustain the long-end real-rate and supply premium beyond the next Fed decision.
watch_events.market_trigger: Weak participation or continued post-auction cheapening would confirm absorption strain.
watch_events.title: Long-end Treasury supply test
watch_events.watch_line: Stops, bidder awards, dealer retention, and secondary trading will measure duration capacity.

watch_events.market_implication: That combination would keep US benchmark and long yields exposed even if domestic data soften.
watch_events.market_trigger: A tighter RBNZ, firmer Chinese prices, or weak JGB demand with broader wage pressure.
watch_events.title: Global inflation-duration test
watch_events.watch_line: RBNZ guidance, Chinese prices, and Japan’s assessment and auction will test cross-market pressure.
